title: jwt.verify accepts the "none" algorithm — signature verification can be bypassed

params.passDetail: jwt.verify's algorithms allow-list does not include "none" — unsigned tokens are rejected.
params.failDetail: jwt.verify is called with "none" in its algorithms allow-list. An attacker can forge a token with alg: none and no signature, and it will be accepted — full authentication bypass. Remove "none" from algorithms; pin to the real signing algorithm(s) (e.g. ["RS256"]).
params.absentCallDetail: No jwt.verify call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: jwt.verify does not pass an algorithms allow-list literal; verify the resolved list excludes "none".

provenance.note: The alg:none / algorithm-confusion bypass is a canonical JWT footgun. Detecting it needs an ARRAY-aware checker — the shape this proposal adds.
